title: SentinelBench Incident
description: Canonical schema for a single incident/scenario record used by SentinelBench scenarios and scorers. An incident is one unit of evaluation: a bundle of raw events, ground-truth labels, and the evidence-grounded expectations an agent should be scored against.

incident_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this incident within SentinelBench. Recommended format: '<dataset-slug>-<zero-padded-number>', e.g. 'sb-000123'.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the record conforms to (semver). Defaults to the current schema version if omitted.

event_source.description: Provenance of the raw events: where they came from and how they were collected or generated.

name.description: Human-readable name of the specific source/product/dataset, e.g. 'Zeek conn.log', 'AWS CloudTrail', 'synthetic-generator-v1'.

license.description: License or terms under which this data is redistributed. Required for any non-synthetic source per data/README.md (approved public datasets only).

provenance_url.description: Link documenting where this data came from (dataset page, paper, repo).

raw_events.description: Ordered list of raw events that make up the evidence for this incident. Kept intentionally loose in shape since source formats vary (EDR telemetry, syslog, CloudTrail JSON, etc.) — each event must at least carry an id and timestamp so it can be referenced from supporting_event_ids and investigation/containment steps.

event_id.description: Identifier for this event, unique within the incident. Referenced by supporting_event_ids and by steps below.

raw.description: The original event payload, kept as close to source format as practical (object or string).

severity.description: Ground-truth severity, aligned to a standard 5-level scale. Meaningful even for benign incidents that resemble malicious activity closely enough to warrant analyst attention (e.g. 'informational').

attack_techniques.description: MITRE ATT&CK techniques (and optionally sub-techniques) relevant to this incident. Empty array is valid for benign incidents with no adversarial technique involved.

technique_id.description: ATT&CK technique or sub-technique ID, e.g. 'T1059' or 'T1059.001'.

technique_name.description: Human-readable ATT&CK technique name, e.g. 'Command and Scripting Interpreter'.

tactic.description: ATT&CK tactic this technique maps to in this incident, e.g. 'execution', 'persistence'.

supporting_event_ids.description: event_id values from raw_events that constitute the evidence trail for the label/severity/technique determination. Every id here must correspond to an entry in raw_events.

expected_investigation_steps.description: Ordered, evidence-grounded steps a competent analyst/agent should take to investigate this incident. Used by scorers to evaluate whether an agent's investigation was correct and sufficiently grounded in the raw events.

step.description: Concise description of the investigative action, e.g. 'Pivot on parent process ID to identify the spawning process.'

rationale.description: Why this step is expected, tying it back to the evidence.

supporting_event_ids.description: Subset of raw_events/event_ids this step relies on, if applicable.

expected_containment_actions.description: Expected containment/remediation actions once the incident is confirmed. For benign incidents, this may reasonably be an empty array (i.e. the correct action is to take no containment action).

action.description: Concise description of the containment/remediation action, e.g. 'Isolate host from network' or 'Revoke session tokens for compromised identity.'

rationale.description: Why this action is appropriate given the confirmed incident.

priority.description: Urgency of this action relative to other containment actions.

metadata.description: Optional free-form metadata (author, creation date, tags, dataset split, etc.) that doesn't affect scoring.
